title: AI Systems Engineer
description: **About Platform Engineering** Platform Engineering (platformengineering.org) is home to the world's largest community of platform engineers. Through events like PlatformCon, which brings together over 35,000\+ participants every year, we connect thousands of professionals, share best practices, and help shape the future of how teams build and scale internal developer platforms. We're looking for an AI Systems Engineer to own and evolve the systems and data infrastructure that power our operations, from CRM pipelines and integrations to the technical backbone of our content production engine. This position is fully remote and is open to applicants based in Europe. ****What You Will Do**** * Own and evolve our data infrastructure: Supabase, n8n workflows, HubSpot integrations, and the pipelines connecting external platforms like Skilljar and HockeyStack. * Take technical ownership of our content factory: tooling, automation pipelines, and agent orchestration that let a small team produce at scale. * Think through the user journey and create UI integrations into existing solutions (e.g., Airtable) or buy or build and maintain simple UI interfaces for databases, etc. (search, export data, import to other workflows). * Keep our stack modular and replaceable, evaluating tools continuously and making changes before we become dependent on the wrong ones. * Reduce manual overhead systematically by building automations and agent workflows that replace repetitive processes. * Work with leadership as a peer on architectural decisions and infrastructure direction, not just as an implementer. ****What We're Looking For**** * Strong technical background: database schemas, REST/GraphQL API integrations, vector databases, and automation pipelines, end\-to\-end and without hand\-holding. * Fluency with AI as a development tool. You use Claude Code, Cursor, or similar and actively think about where agents can replace manual work. * Commercial mindset and strong prioritization: you know which systems move the needle and focus there. * Senior enough to own a roadmap and hold architecture conversations with leadership. * Bonus: a background in software development, experience with HubSpot APIs, AI agent frameworks, or marketing/content automation. ****Why You'll Love Working With Us**** * Your work will directly enable a community of 35,000\+ platform engineers worldwide. * Real ownership of core infrastructure, with the autonomy to shape it. * Fully remote across Europe, strong async culture, low\-ego team. * Competitive salary and room to grow as our ambitions scale. If this sounds like a fit, we would love to hear from you! Have questions before applying? Reach out to us at **careers@platformengineering.org**

title: Account Executive - UKI Market - Talent Solutions
description: LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed. Join us to transform the way the world works. **Job Description** At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centred on trust and optimised for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. LinkedIn was built to help professionals achieve more in their careers, and every day millions of people use our products to make connections, discover opportunities and gain insights. Our global reach means we get to make a direct impact on the world’s workforce in ways no other company can. We transform lives through innovative products and technology. We are looking for an Account Executive (Talent Solutions) to join our new business sales team in tapping into potential client opportunities and generating new business. You will be responsible for driving an outbound prospecting strategy in your territory. You will strategically approach target companies and teams, identify the best technology options and sell corporate solutions for hiring and talent development. What You’ll Do: * Prospect relentlessly to build pipeline and build strong personal relationships with prospects * Create reliable forecasts and be completely transparent with management on the pipeline status * Close new business consistently at or above quota level * Develop and execute on a strategic plan for the territory and document and distribute competitive information * Invest in colleagues and give coaching and advice when you see an opportunity for improvement * Work to develop and circulate the set of best practices that will be the foundation of this team What Success Looks Like: * Listen to the needs of the market and share insights with product and marketing teams * Be proactive about solving problems even if it’s outside of your area and be ready to take on additional initiatives and responsibilities as they emerge * Seek out opportunities to be a leader and do everything you can to help the company achieve its larger objectives Qualifications **Basic Qualifications:** * 2\+ years of applicable sales experience * Business fluency in English **Preferred Qualifications:** * 1\+ year(s) closing sales experience * Experience in HR Tech and recruitment solutions selling * Experience with SaaS opportunities and Salesforce.com platform and/or Dynamics CRM * Knowledge of software contract terms and conditions with the ability to create fair transactions * Strong negotiation and accurate forecasting skills * Demonstrated ability to find, manage and close high\-level business in an evangelist sales environment * Ability to assess business opportunities and use data to inform decision making and persuade others * Ability to manage a large number of prospect situations simultaneously while positioning company products against direct and indirect **Suggested Skills:** * Negotiation * Forecasting * Communication * Recruiting **Additional Information** **Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates ​** Please follow this link to access the document that provides transparency around the way in which LinkedIn handles personal data of employees and job applicants: https://legal.linkedin.com/candidate\-portal.

title: Accounts Receivable Accountant
description: **Position** : Accounts Receivable Accountant **Location** : Dublin or Stockholm **Move Security Forward with Acre** Are you passionate about transforming the future of security? Do you thrive in a collaborative, innovative environment where your contributions drive real impact? If so, Acre Security is the place for you. Join us in making the world a safer place — one innovation at a time. **About Acre** At Acre, we're not just building security solutions — we’re empowering security professionals to adapt and thrive in a rapidly changing world. Since our inception in 2012, Acre has been at the forefront of innovation in access control, visitor management, and intrusion software. Our solutions are trusted by organizations in over 25 countries, delivering smarter, more adaptable, and easier\-to\-use security technologies that protect what matters most. **Your Impact** We’re looking for a talented **Accounts Receivable Accountant** to join our Finance team. In this role, you will: * Own and manage the Accounts Receivable function, using NetSuite to ensure accurate allocation of customer payments, account reconciliations, and strong cash collection performance across customer accounts. * Be a key member of the Finance team supporting month\-end reporting and financial accuracy * Partner with Commercial teams to resolve billing queries and improve debtor processes * Drive continuous improvement within AR operations by identifying efficiencies, strengthening controls, and helping maintain healthy cash flow and customer relationships. **What You’ll Do** In addition to the above, your key responsibilities will include: * Booking of incoming customer payments to the bank and allocation to customer accounts * Reconciliation of Accounts Receivable accounts * Support month\-end close activities, including AR reporting, journals, and account reconciliations * Build strong working relationships with customers to resolve billing and payment queries efficiently * Creation/editing of customer accounts on NetSuite * Liaise with Credit Manager in relation to credit limits, credit terms and over\-due customer invoices * Issue credit notes * Assist with audits and provide supporting documentation as required * Contribute to wider finance team activities and ad hoc accounting projects when required **What You'll Bring** To succeed in this role, you'll need: * 5\+ years’ experience in Accounts Receivable, Credit Control, or a similar finance role. * Strong understanding of AR processes, reconciliations, and month\-end finance activities * Excellent attention to detail with a high level of accuracy and organisation * Experience using ERP or accounting systems and strong Excel skills * Strong communication and relationship\-management skills, with the ability to work effectively across teams and with external customers * Relevant finance or accounting qualification (part\-qualified or qualified by experience considered) * A proactive mindset with a focus on problem\-solving and continuous improvement **What’s In It For You** We strive to do more than just match your ambitions — when you join Acre, you’ll have access to a range of exciting opportunities: * Be part of a disruptor – Help move security forward with a company that challenges the status quo and redefines industry standards * Learn from industry leaders – Work alongside experts and innovators driving real change in security technology * Drive meaningful impact – Your work will directly contribute to pioneering security technologies that protect businesses and communities worldwide * Advance your career – Access professional development opportunities, including training programs, mentorship, and industry events * Enjoy competitive rewards – We offer a competitive compensation package, performance\-based incentives, and a comprehensive benefits program that reflects your contributions **Join the Future of Security** At Acre, we’re moving security forward. If you're ready to make an impact in a fast\-moving, innovative industry, we’d love to hear from you. **Acre is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.** **Data Privacy Statement** We process personal data submitted in connection with your application for recruitment purposes in accordance with applicable data protection laws. Applicant data will generally be retained for up to 12 months from the date of last activity unless otherwise required by law or where consent has been provided for future opportunities. For details, please see our Privacy Notice: Acre Security Powered by JazzHR m6PBEkjti1

title: Analyst - Collab Tech
description: **About Kerry** --------------- Kerry is the world's leading taste and nutrition company for the food, beverage and pharmaceutical industries. Every day we partner with customers to create healthier, tastier and more sustainable products that are consumed by billions of people across the world. Our vision is to be our customers' most valued partner, creating a world of sustainable nutrition. A career with Kerry offers you an opportunity to shape the future of food while providing you opportunities to explore and grow in a truly global environment.**About the role** ------------------ Here at Kerry, we currently have an opportunity for the role of Business Analyst (BA) within our Collaboration Technology team. The Collab Tech team are responsible for ensuring there is a robust collaboration platform for Kerry’s employees and the BA will be responsible for engaging with representatives across the organisation to add value and address business needs through the use of our Collaboration Portfolio including SharePoint, Microsoft Teams, Power Automate, PowerApps, Copilot and other related Microsoft technologies. The successful Candidate will report to the Collab Tech Solution Manager within the ICT function. Kerry operates a Hybrid working policy \- candidates will be required to work from our offices in Naas 3 days per week. **Key responsibilities** ------------------------ The core responsibilities will include: * Engage with stakeholders to collect, evaluate, and document business requirements, while providing informed recommendations on feasible digital solutions. * Partner with business stakeholders to bridge the gap between IT and business functions, effectively conveying business requirements to technical teams and translating technical solutions for business audiences. * Conducting thorough testing of digital solutions to verify alignment with business requirements and expected functionality, including the development of test scripts, documentation of test evidence, and management of defect resolution through to closure. * Proactive ownership of projects in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment, including monitoring and reporting on project progress, risks and issues. * Work with our Security, Data Governance, and Service Delivery teams to implement digital projects in compliance with ICT security and infrastructure standards. * Support cutover activities including Data Migration activities and Post Go\-live Support * Provide application support including troubleshooting issues and ensure timely resolution of issues. * Evaluate new solutions and technologies as they become available and relevant for Kerry. **Qualifications and skills** ----------------------------- In Kerry we understand that people have very different career trajectories and experiences. To be successful in this role we would be looking for the right competencies and aptitudes as well as your proven track record of success in a similar role and environment. These include: * Degree in ICT, Business or other related discipline * Business Analyst experience or in a similar role, successfully managing and delivering projects using established methodologies to ensure successful end\-to\-end delivery. * Experience with Sharepoint or Power Platform administration desired. * Strong experience acting as a trusted advisor to internal stakeholders. * Excellent document writing skills (e.g. business requirements, functional specifications, business process design documents) * Excellent communicator with experience of managing relationships with a range of stakeholders, including those at a senior level * Strong project management capabilities with a proven track record of implementing new business processes * Strong analytical skills with keen attention to detail * Solutions focused with a proven track record as a problem solver * Bias for action and desire to continuously improve ways of working **Get in touch today!** ----------------------- In Kerry we benefit from the knowledge of our colleagues who bring a diverse range of cultures, backgrounds, lifestyles, and experiences. One team fostering an inclusive culture that, above all, inspires food and nourishes life. One culture where everyone brings their unique perspectives and experiences to help make us better, together. We are committed to nurturing an environment of positivity and inclusiveness, where everyone can be at their best, both personally and professionally. Our recruitment, selection and assessment process are based on the skills and competencies of the specific roles and based entirely on merit. We are committed to and value Diversity and Inclusion in all recruitment processes within Kerry and do not discriminate based on gender, race, class, economic status, ethnic background, sexual orientation, age, political beliefs, veteran status, marital status or any other protected characteristic. Beware of scams online or from individuals claiming to represent us. A Kerry employee will not solicit candidates through a non\-Kerry email address or phone number. In addition, Kerry does not currently utilise video chat rooms (e.g., Google Hangouts) to conduct interviews. Refuse any request that asks you to provide payment to participate in the hiring process (e.g., purchasing a “starter kit,” investing in training, or something similar). Kerry will not ask you to pay any money at any point in the hiring process with the exception of reimbursable travel expenses. In addition, any payments made by Kerry will be from official firm accounts bearing the Kerry name. **Please note: We do not accept CVs or candidate profiles from recruitment agencies where Kerry terms of business have not been signed. Additionally, we will not consider or agree to payment of any recruiter fee under these circumstances. This also applies to CVs or candidate profiles sent directly to any Kerry Hiring Managers.**

title: Anti-Piracy Intelligence Analyst
description: Sky's Anti\-Piracy team is at the forefront of tackling one of the most significant challenges facing the television and creative industries\-content piracy. Sky invests billions of euros in high\-quality entertainment, reflecting the value our customers place on premium content. Protecting this investment is critical, and we work closely with government, media, and technology partners to safeguard intellectual property and ensure audiences can continue to enjoy world\-class content now and in the future. To combat piracy effectively, the Anti\-Piracy team undertakes a wide range of targeted activities addressing the evolving piracy landscape, the individuals involved, and the infrastructure that enables it. This includes deploying technological solutions, conducting intelligence\-led investigations, supporting law enforcement referrals, pursuing legal action, and disrupting underlying infrastructure. The Intelligence Analyst plays a central role in this effort, responsible for collecting, processing, and analysing intelligence relating to piracy in Ireland. Using a range of analytical tools and methodologies, the Analyst will generate actionable insights, identify key actors, and support the development of a consistent pipeline of investigative opportunities. The role also provides critical support to Senior Intelligence Analysts and Investigators, contributing to strategic intelligence requests, network analysis, landscape mapping, and ongoing investigative activity. The Irish Anti\-Piracy team comprises dedicated investigators and intelligence analysts, supported by wider Group function s including Digital Forensics, Insights, Content Protection, Communications, and Anti\-Piracy Labs, all based in London. **What you'll do*** Learn and understand the key aspects of piracy and the methodologies and technology the pirates use to conduct their offending. * Gather piracy intelligence from a range of sources and ingest it into our intelligence database. * Support the team through the processing of intelligence from ongoing investigations and new targets. * Triage and deconflict incoming intelligence. * Work up actionable target profiles to bring to Tasking for the investigators. * Use the intelligence system to identify patterns, problem behaviours , key individuals and locations. * Work closely with other members of the team to produce key intelligence products such as landscape maps, network charts and threat assessments, to allow us priorities at strategic, tactical, and operational levels * Learn and understand intelligence grading systems so that intelligence produced is standardized, suitable for dissemination, and aids with prioritization. * Identify gaps in our intelligence and produce Intelligence Requests to fill those gaps. * Continuously seek to improve the efficiency of our processes and the output of team. * Be inquisitive and be comfortable challenging hypothesis and decisions, to ensure that the highest standards are reached. * Support and be a part of projects across the department which contribute towards better systems, data, and processes. **What you'll bring"** Essential criteria:" * Experience in an intelligence , investigations, cyber, fraud, digital risk, or analytical role. * Open\-source intelligence research skills. * Experience collecting, evaluating, and analysing information from multiple sources to produc e clear, structured, and actionable reports. * High attention to detail and ability to critically analyze information , draw meaningful conclusions , and identify patterns and links across fragmented data sets. * Track record of personal integrity, discretion, and professionalism. Desirable skills and experience: " * Knowledge of Sky's products and services and an awareness of current industry trends * Knowledge of iBase and I2 Analyst Notebook (or other intelligence database systems) is desirable but not essential as training will be provided. * Excellent working knowledge of excel, word and powerpoint . * Strong written and verbal communication skills . * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively as part of a wider team * Desire to continuously develop and improve. **Benefits and perks** " There's one thing people can't stop talking about when it comes to life at Sky: the perks . Here's a taster:" " * Free Sky TV package, including Sky Sports and Sky Cinema"" " * A generous pension package * Private healthcare with mental health support" " * Dental insurance" " * Discounts on Sky products, including Sky M obile, Sky B roadband, Sky Glass and Sky Protect" " * Sharesave and Tech schemes" " * A range of Sky VIP rewards and experiences " **How you'll work** " We've adopted a hybrid working approach to give more flexibility on where and how we work. The hybrid working expectations for this role are 3 days in the office per week. " " **Your office base"** Burlington Plaza is the home of Sky Ireland, a central, well \- connected base for our Dublin teams. We're just a 20\-minute walk to Dublin's city centre with connections via Dublin Bus, Luas and DART. Our nearest train station is Grand Canal Dock." **Who we are** " We're Sky, a leading media and entertainment company who connect millions with entertainment, sports, news and arts through innovative products and services. Working with us means you'll be bringing the joy of a better experience to more people, every day. All so we can do better and deliver better for our customers, colleagues and society . " We're an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. title: Apprentice Plumber (3rd/4th year)
description: **Competitive Hourly Rate \| Full\-Time \| Weekly Pay \| Excellent Benefits** Churchfield Home Services is seeking an **Apprentice Plumber** to join our growing retrofit and energy\-efficiency team in the **Dublin region**. This is an excellent opportunity to build a long\-term trade career while working alongside experienced plumbers on real, high\-quality projects. You’ll learn the practical, safety, and technical skills required to qualify as a plumber, while contributing to the delivery of modern, low\-carbon heating and plumbing systems in Irish homes. At Churchfield, apprentices are supported, trained properly, and treated as valued team members — not cheap labour. **What You’ll Do** * Assist qualified plumbers and supervisors with plumbing and heating installations * Prepare tools, materials, and work areas in line with RAMS * Carry out basic plumbing tasks under supervision (pipe cutting, fitting, fixing supports) * Learn correct installation methods for retrofit plumbing and heating systems * Support testing, commissioning, and handover activities as directed * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe work environment at all times * Participate in toolbox talks, on\-site coaching, and safety briefings * Follow CHS SOPs, quality standards, and site instructions exactly as trained * Handle materials responsibly and support correct waste segregation **What You’ll Need** * Registered Plumbing Apprenticeship with SOLAS (or actively working towards registration) * Willingness to learn, follow instructions, and develop technical skills * Strong attention to safety and quality * Reliable, punctual, and team\-oriented attitude * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling (or willingness to complete) * Full, clean B driving licence * Fluent English (spoken and written) **Pay \& Benefits** * Competitive hourly rate * Pension (CWPS): Includes Income Protection \+ €150,000 Death\-in\-Service * Annual Leave: 20 days \+ 10 public holidays * Meal Allowance: €5/day (revenue\-compliant) * Sick, Maternity \& Paternity Pay, Bereavement Leave * Service Awards and Bonus Vouchers * Bike\-to\-Work Scheme, Employee Referral Bonus, and Team Events **Career Growth** Clear progression to **Qualified Plumber**, with opportunities to advance into Plumbing Supervisor Supervisor, or Senior Plumbing Supervisor roles for apprentices who demonstrate commitment, reliability, and strong workmanship. Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Food allowance * Sick pay Application question(s): * Are you a registered Apprentice Plumber with SOLAS? title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
description: We are partnering with a major global financial institution to build a pioneering, greenfield AI engineering team from the ground up in South Dublin (hybrid 3 days per week on\-site) to hire a Senior AI Engineer / Python AI Engineer for an initial 6\-18 month contract. This team is tasked with moving beyond simple chat interfaces to design, build, and deploy the next generation of **Agentic AI workflows** and autonomous systems. As a foundational engineer, you will focus on initial Proof of Concepts (PoCs) and core architecture, transforming complex financial business problems into highly resilient AI solutions. **What You Will Do** * **Design Agentic Systems:** Architect multi\-agent systems, complex cognitive routing loops, and human\-in\-the\-loop validation layers. * **Build Durable Workflows:** Implement resilient, fault\-tolerant orchestrations for long\-running AI tasks that must survive service interruptions. * **Develop Greenfield Apps:** Write clean, production\-grade Python code to take experimental AI models from prototype to enterprise\-ready solutions. * **Collaborate Directly:** Work side\-by\-side with tech leadership in a high\-impact, fast\-paced R\&D environment. **What We Are Looking For** We recognize that the intersection of Agentic AI and Durable Execution is a highly progressive space. If you don't have the exact tools listed but have built equivalent architectures, we want to hear from you. * **The AI Brain:** Deep expertise in Python and LLM frameworks like **LangGraph** , **LangChain** , **CrewAI** , or **AutoGen** . Experience building stateful, cyclic, or multi\-agent systems is highly valued. * **The Infrastructure Muscle:** Strong experience with durable execution engines or complex state orchestration using **Temporal** , **Cadence** , **Inngest** , or **AWS Step Functions** . * **Core Systems Design:** A solid understanding of advanced backend patterns, including **Event\-Driven Architectures** , **State Machines** , **Idempotency** , and the **Saga Pattern** . * **Industry Context:** Previous experience navigating high\-security, high\-throughput environments (FinTech, Banking, or large scale Enterprise SaaS) is a major plus. **How to Apply** If you are a Senior Python/AI engineer who thrives in high\-impact environments and wants to shape the future of enterprise AI, apply directly here or send your CV/Resume

title: Brands Event Coordinator
description: ***Join Tether and Shape the Future of Digital Finance*** At Tether, we’re not just building products, we’re pioneering a global financial revolution. Our cutting\-edge solutions empower businesses—from exchanges and wallets to payment processors and ATMs—to seamlessly integrate reserve\-backed tokens across blockchains. By harnessing the power of blockchain technology, Tether enables you to store, send, and receive digital tokens instantly, securely, and globally, all at a fraction of the cost. Transparency is the bedrock of everything we do, ensuring trust in every transaction. ***Innovate with Tether*** **Tether Finance:** Our innovative product suite features the world’s most trusted stablecoin, **USDT**, relied upon by hundreds of millions worldwide, alongside pioneering digital asset tokenization services. But that’s just the beginning: **Tether Power:** Driving sustainable growth, our energy solutions optimize excess power for Bitcoin mining using eco\-friendly practices in state\-of\-the\-art, geo\-diverse facilities. **Tether Data:** Fueling breakthroughs in AI and peer\-to\-peer technology, we reduce infrastructure costs and enhance global communications with cutting\-edge solutions like **KEET**, our flagship app that redefines secure and private data sharing. **Tether Education**: Democratizing access to top\-tier digital learning, we empower individuals to thrive in the digital and gig economies, driving global growth and opportunity. **Tether Evolution**: At the intersection of technology and human potential, we are pushing the boundaries of what is possible, crafting a future where innovation and human capabilities merge in powerful, unprecedented ways. ***Why Join Us?*** Our team is a global talent powerhouse, working remotely from every corner of the world. If you’re passionate about making a mark in the fintech space, this is your opportunity to collaborate with some of the brightest minds, pushing boundaries and setting new standards. We’ve grown fast, stayed lean, and secured our place as a leader in the industry. If you have excellent English communication skills and are ready to contribute to the most innovative platform on the planet, Tether is the place for you. **Are you ready to be part of the future?** **About the job** We are looking for a versatile and high\-energy Brands Event Coordinator to manage a diverse portfolio of events and brand\-specific activations and events. In this role, you will coordinate high\-impact events for our various internal brands—ensuring each one reflects our world\-class standards while maintaining its own unique brand identity. You won't just be working solo; you will be a vital part of our global events engine. When you aren't leading your own brand events, you will be the "right hand" to our senior team members on major international events. **Responsibilities** * **End\-to\-End Execution:** Lead the coordination of international events from initial concept and booth design through to on\-site execution and post\-event reporting. * **Stakeholder Management:** Act as the primary liaison between global venues, vendors, and internal stakeholders to ensure seamless service delivery. * **Logistics \& Operations**: Master the "fine print" by managing complex calendars, scheduling cross\-functional meetings, overseeing intricate travel, event logistics, Finance \& Budget coordination. * **Financial Oversight**: Support the Expansion Team with budget reconciliation, expense tracking, and detailed performance reporting. * Internal **Logistics**: Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate travel logistics. * **Global Communication**: Manage both internal and external communications, ensuring the brand voice is consistent across English and Spanish\-speaking markets. * Bachelor’s Degree in Event Management or Bachelor’s Degree in Hospitality \& Tourism Management * A willingness and availability to travel when required. * Experience: 5\+ years of proven success in global corporate events or the professional event industry. * Linguistic Versatility: Native or full professional proficiency in English and Spanish is required. (Bonus points if you speak Italian). * Tech\-Savviness: Advanced proficiency in Google Suite and Microsoft Office, with the ability to quickly adopt new project management tools. * A solid grasp of budgeting, invoicing, and expense management. * Adaptability: A "no task too big or small" mindset, comfortable moving from high\-level sponsorship strategy to the ground\-level execution. **Important information for candidates** Recruitment scams have become increasingly common. To protect yourself, please keep the following in mind when applying for roles: * **Apply only through our official channels.** We do not use third\-party platforms or agencies for recruitment unless clearly stated. All open roles are listed on our official careers page: https://tether.recruitee.com/ * **Verify the recruiter’s identity.** All our recruiters have verified LinkedIn profiles. If you’re unsure, you can confirm their identity by checking their profile or contacting us through our website. * **Be cautious of unusual communication methods.** We do not conduct interviews over WhatsApp, Telegram, or SMS. All communication is done through official company emails and platforms. * **Double\-check email addresses.** All communication from us will come from emails ending in **@**tether.to **or @**tether.io * **We will never request payment or financial details.** If someone asks for personal financial information or payment at any point during the hiring process, it is a scam. Please report it immediately. **When in doubt, feel free to reach out through our official website.**

title: CM: ESB Trainee Solicitor 2026
description: Vacancy Number: 28579 Category: Apprentice \- Business Job Type: Temporary Full\-time **Trainee Solicitor Contract with the ESB in\-house Legal Team** **Position Description** ESB’s in\-house Legal Department plays a pivotal role in delivering ESB’s *Net Zero by 2040* strategy. The team advises across all of ESB’s businesses in electricity generation, supply, and transmission, providing expert legal support in six core practice areas: Corporate \& Commercial, Regulatory \& Competition, Employment \& Industrial Relations, Litigation \& Dispute Resolution, Commercial Property, and Planning \& Environmental law. As a member of the in\-house legal team, the trainee solicitor will contribute directly to the delivery of ESB’s strategy, which is anchored in ESB’s ambition to lead the national transition to a low‑carbon energy future based on clean, reliable, and affordable electricity. ESB’s strategy involves significant capital investment across multiple complex projects, requiring the provision of timely, high‑quality legal advice and transactional support. The trainee solicitor will work across the ESB Legal Department, undertaking rotations in a range of the practice areas outlined above. During each legal practice rotation, the trainee solicitor will report to a senior solicitor within the relevant practice area. In addition, a designated training solicitor will be appointed to provide ongoing guidance, support, and supervision throughout the training contract. **Key Responsibilities of this role** The responsibilities of the successful candidate will include: * Working proactively and collaboratively with the senior practice area solicitor or a delegated solicitor to provide high quality and timely legal advice to internal clients across ESB Group * Assisting in the management of files to ensure that client’s needs are attended to in a prompt manner with clear high quality legal advice * Assisting with the drafting of correspondence and contracts * Research and advisory work in collaboration with senior practice area solicitor * Meeting with and advising ESB internal clients in collaboration with the senior practice area solicitor * Working in compliance with and ensuring adherence with ESB’s corporate governance frameworks * Liaising with other specialist areas within ESB as required * Assisting in maintaining positive and collaborative relationships with internal clients, the ESB in\-house legal team, external law firms and counsel appointed by ESB * Keeping appraised of legislative changes and case law **Essential Skills, Experience and Qualifications** The successful candidate will: * Demonstrate strong organisational and analytical skills * Possess effective communication and interpersonal abilities * Show excellent legal research and drafting capability * Work collaboratively and build strong relationships with clients and colleagues * Act with integrity and professionalism * Perform effectively under pressure and meet tight deadlines * Be proficient in Microsoft 365 **Qualification** On or before 30th July 2026: Has successfully passed all eight Law Society of Ireland FE1 entrance examinations and meet the Law Society of Ireland’s requirements for acceptance onto its Professional Practice Course 2026, commencing in September 2026 and passed all entry requirements to the Law Society After completion of the Professional Practice Course 2026 the trainee solicitor will be required to undertake the advanced electives. The training solicitor will provide guidance to the trainee solicitor on the electives that align best with ESB’s Driven to make a difference Net Zero by 2040 strategy. Subject to the terms of agreement between ESB and the trainee solicitor ESB will make payment of the trainee solicitors Professional Practice Course and Advanced Elective fees. The trainee solicitor will also receive a salary while undertaking the Professional Practice Course 2026\. **Location** The base location for this role will be ESB Networks Office, Leopardstown Road and/or ESB Head Office, 27 Fitzwilliam Street Lower, Dublin 2, D02KT92\. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week**. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Closing Date for applications:** **21st June 2026** **Salary:** **€39,000** ***Please advise if you require any additional accommodations to assist you if you are called to attend at interview.*** ***Note: Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment******@esb.ie*** ***ESB is an equal opportunity employer*** **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Chemistry Expert (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Experts with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that help train cutting\-edge AI models to think like scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working researchers, postdocs, faculty, and industry scientists who want meaningful work on their own schedule. No prior AI experience needed — just deep chemistry knowledge and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve advanced chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, and computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated scientific content for accuracy, depth, and reasoning quality * Develop complex, real\-world problem statements that challenge and improve AI model reasoning * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts * Ensure scientific rigor, clarity, and precision across all deliverables * Apply computational tools or coding skills where relevant to problem design and validation **Who You Are** * Hold a Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one area: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with Python or computational chemistry software (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong scientific communication skills * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously * Fluent in English **Nice to Have** * Experience with data annotation, scientific evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Background in AI, machine learning, or cheminformatics * Familiarity with molecular modeling, reaction mechanisms, or materials science * Experience writing for scientific publication or peer review **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs and AI teams * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task\-based scientific work * Apply your expertise to problems that directly influence how AI understands science * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Chief Executive Officer
description: **About The Role** Parentline, Ireland's national helpline for parents, is seeking a new CEO to lead the organisation into its next chapter. This is a hands\-on, wide\-ranging role with real autonomy, where you will be the public face, operational lead and strategic driver of a national charity, ensuring that no parent in Ireland feels alone or unsupported. Working closely with a supportive and engaged Board, you will lead a high\-impact team including over 60 dedicated volunteers, overseeing service delivery, volunteer management, fundraising and communications. A true people person, the ideal candidate will be a warm and values\-driven leader who is passionate about making a meaningful difference in the lives of parents and families across Ireland. **For more information, please download a detailed candidate briefing document here.** **About Parentline** Parentline is Ireland's national helpline for parents, guardians and carers. For 43 years it has provided free, confidential and non\-judgemental support to parents navigating every aspect of raising children. In that time, it has supported over 160,000 parents across Ireland. Along with the Helpline, Parentline offers a suite of targeted support programmes including the Non\-Violent Resistance Programme (NVR) for families experiencing child to parent violence, the Supportive Parenting for Anxious Childhood Emotions Programme (SPACE) for families dealing with childhood anxiety and Parents Plus parenting courses. For more information, please visit www.parentline.ie. **How To Apply** 2into3 are managing the search and selection process for this position on an exclusive retained basis. Please contact **Orla Dolan, Consultant – Talent** for confidential enquiries in relation to this role at **orla.dolan@2into3\.com / \+353 86 792 4696\.** Please note this role is offered on a 32\-hour per week contract. Applications that do not have a cover letter unfortunately cannot be considered.***

title: Clinical Tutor – HST Institute of Medicine
company: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
description: **Company Description** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care. Founded in 1654, the college supports doctors to enhance their skills, competencies, and professionalism throughout their working lives. Through its Faculties and Institutes, The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, offers medical training programmes, examinations, diplomas, and educational courses to equip doctors with the skills and knowledge they need to care for patients and run safe and efficient healthcare systems. It also offers a range of educational diplomas and courses for learners in the wider healthcare sector **Job Description** **LOCATION:** Hybrid/Remote – Work from home/ RCPI, 19 Frederick Street South, Dublin 2 **REPORTS TO:** Manager, Education Delivery **CONTRACT TYPE:** 2\-year Specific Purpose contract **POSITION:** Depending on specialty, days may vary. 6 days max per Anum. **SALARY:** €500 per day pro rata **CLOSING DATE:** 12 June at 16:00 Objective The principal objective of this post is to participate in the facilitation of tutorials for RCPI HST Institute of Medicine Trainees as part of the Taught Programme of learning for the 2026\-2027 academic year. The HST Clinical Tutor will be responsible for the facilitation of tutorials to HST Trainees in assigned tutorial groups. HST tutorials will be delivered biannually. Each tutorial will be hosted on zoom, within RCPI Brightspace virtual learning environment. EG: HST YEAR 3 * Tutorial 1: Planning Interventions to Decrease Inequity * Tutorial 2: Leading for Quality and Patient Safety In advance of each tutorial, RCPI will hold a training session for all clinical tutors on the specific content for the upcoming tutorial. Each tutorial group will also be provided with access to a discussion board. Moderation of this discussion board will be the responsibility of the Clinical Tutor. Full training on RCPI systems and tutorial topics will be provided. All tutors have an assigned RCPI Learning Experience Designer. Responsibilities of the role include. * Attend all onboarding and briefing sessions. * Contextualise and adapt standard tutorial templates and resources for the tutorial group with the RCPI Learning Experience Designer * Facilitate tutorials as per agreed schedule. * Contribute to, and moderate, tutorial group discussion boards. * Provide academic support to HST Trainees * Forge relationships with your HST Trainees, encouraging their interaction at tutorials and within their peer group. * Monitor Trainee progress, attendance, and conduct. * Identify potential Trainees in difficulty, monitoring any shortfalls in attendance. * Keep detailed records of serious issues or breach codes of conduct arise. * Ensure that all Royal College of Physicians of Ireland policies and procedures are adhered too. * Other duties as may be assigned by the RCPI Education Function **Requirements** Essential to Teach Year 3 HST tutorials * Recognised Medical Degree (MB BCh BAO or MB BS) * The appointee must be in good standing with the General Medical Register of the Irish Medical Council * Appropriate level of experience * Consultant with experience in the tutorial topics * Senior SpRs / Specialist Registrar with experience in the tutorial topics * Public health specialist or clinician with quality improvement leadership role * Excellent teaching/facilitation and communication skills * Computer literacy – adept at Microsoft suite * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * Enthusiasm and flexibility Essential to teach Year 1\&2 HST tutorials * Recognised Medical Degree (MB BCh BAO or MB BS) * The appointee must be in good standing with the General Medical Register of the Irish Medical Council * Relevant clinical experience in a hospital environment, with at least 4 years working at registrar level * Excellent facilitation and communication skills * Computer literacy – adept at Microsoft suite * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * Enthusiasm and flexibility Desirable * Experience with a virtual learning platform (e.g., Brightspace, Canvas, Moodle) * A formal postgraduate qualification (e.g., at master's level) in medical education * MD or PhD awarded or submitted. * Previous experience of teaching and assessing learners in an academic environment * Demonstrated engagement or project involvement in health inequity and/or quality improvement and ability to translate into real clinical experiences Tutor \- Key areas of work Virtual Tutorials Virtual tutorial sessions will be held biannually for HST tutorial groups focusing on the topics introduced in the relevant online content and explored in the discussion forum. Tutors will manage and facilitate these tutorials. The tutorial format will vary, but will focus on case\-based learning, to highlight and discuss best practice, and used to stimulate reflection of the learning from the online content and how this could be applied to the Trainee’s own practice. Discussion Forums Discussion forums are designed to provide a clinical context to help Trainees apply acquired knowledge and to keep them engaged and motivated throughout the programme. Tutors will be key content and discussion moderators. Trainees will be made aware of the Clinical Tutors availability to respond to Trainee queries on the board e.g., once per week on Wednesday evenings. **About Us** The Royal College of Physicians of Ireland is a postgraduate medical training college dedicated to ensuring that doctors have the skills to provide patients with the best possible care.
